title: echium vulgare subsp. pustulatum
intro: Echium vulgare subsp. pustulatum, also known as Viper's Bugloss, is a biennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the borage family. It is rich in medicinal properties, containing compounds like alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Echium vulgare subsp. pustulatum can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, and ointments, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
